title: Task Parallelization Parallel-Ready Plan
goal: Make relay-dev's control plane able to represent and plan multiple safe task-lane leases while keeping actual provider parallel execution gated.
summary: Lift the hard single-active-job state assumption behind an explicit parallel mode, add deterministic batch lease candidate planning, propagate scheduler metadata into lease specs, and expose remaining capacity in read-only summaries.
non_goals: Do not make max_parallel_jobs > 1 the default.
Do not start a provider worker pool.
Do not run provider execution concurrently.
Do not implement isolated workspace or merge-back.
Do not infer same-workspace safety from changed_files alone.
Do not bypass lease fencing or single-writer commit.
Do not remove compatibility fields current_phase, current_task_id, or active_job_id.
Do not rewrite cli.ps1 step; it remains a one-job synchronous path.

implementation_steps.title: Multi-lease capacity model
implementation_steps.goal: Allow run-state to represent more than one active job when task-lane configuration explicitly permits it.
tasks: Normalize task_lane.max_parallel_jobs to an integer >= 1.
Keep task_lane.mode single by default.
Reject additional active jobs in single mode.
Allow additional active jobs in parallel mode up to max_parallel_jobs.
Reject duplicate leasing of the same task.
Keep compatibility active_job_id stable for older readers.
Keep multi-lease allowance limited to explicit test/helper/control-plane paths; no production execution path creates more than one active lease in this slice.
acceptance: task_lane.max_parallel_jobs is normalized to an integer >= 1.
task_lane.mode remains single by default.
Add-RunStateActiveJobLease rejects additional jobs in single mode.
Add-RunStateActiveJobLease allows additional jobs in parallel mode up to max_parallel_jobs.
Duplicate leasing of the same task is rejected.
Compatibility active_job_id remains set to a stable active job id for older readers.
No production execution path creates more than one active lease in this slice.

implementation_steps.title: Batch lease candidate planner
implementation_steps.goal: Add a scheduler helper that derives a deterministic batch of safe dispatch candidates without executing them.
tasks: Return up to remaining lane capacity candidates.
Use task contracts and run-state as canonical inputs.
Respect dependency, stop_leasing, resource-lock, active-job, and serial-safety constraints.
Include task_id, phase, role, selected_task, resource_locks, parallel_safety, and slot_id in candidate rows.
Keep existing Get-NextAction behavior unchanged.
acceptance: Helper returns up to remaining lane capacity candidates.
Candidates come from task contract state, not Phase4 prose batch summaries.
Dependency, stop_leasing, resource-lock, active-job, and serial-safety constraints are respected.
Candidate rows include task_id, phase, role, selected_task, resource_locks, parallel_safety, and slot_id.
Existing Get-NextAction behavior remains unchanged.

implementation_steps.title: Lease metadata propagation
implementation_steps.goal: Make lease specs carry the scheduler constraint metadata needed by later worker slots.
tasks: Allow planned job specs to carry resource_locks, parallel_safety, slot_id, and workspace_id.
Preserve those fields in active_jobs[job_id] when provided.
Include resource locks and parallel safety in lease events where available.
Keep existing single-step callers backwards compatible.
Treat workspace_id as metadata only; do not switch provider working directories, imply workspace isolation, or provide merge-back safety.
acceptance: Planned job specs can carry resource_locks, parallel_safety, slot_id, and workspace_id.
active_jobs[job_id] preserves those fields when provided.
Lease events can include resource locks and parallel safety where available.
No existing single-step caller needs to provide the new fields.
workspace_id is metadata only in this slice and does not invoke isolated workspace or merge-back behavior.

implementation_steps.title: Parallel-ready observability
implementation_steps.goal: Expose remaining capacity and batch-candidate information in read-only summaries.
tasks: Expose capacity_remaining from New-TaskLaneSummary.
Distinguish capacity_full from job_in_progress when slots are full.
Include derived lease_candidates without persisting them.
Display capacity remaining in watch-run and dashboard surfaces.
acceptance: New-TaskLaneSummary exposes capacity_remaining.
Summary distinguishes capacity_full from job_in_progress when slots are full.
Summary can include derived lease_candidates without persisting them.
watch-run and dashboard surfaces can display capacity remaining.

implementation_steps.title: Parallel-ready regression coverage
implementation_steps.goal: Prove the system is parallel-ready but not yet running providers concurrently.
tasks: Test two independent ready tasks selected as lease candidates when mode=parallel and capacity is 2.
Test two leases can coexist in run-state under parallel mode.
Test same task cannot be leased twice.
Test resource-lock conflicts are excluded from the candidate batch.
Test serial tasks block co-dispatch.
Test Get-NextAction still returns one dispatch action.
Test existing cli.ps1 step cannot dispatch or execute more than one provider job even when fixture state uses task_lane.mode=parallel.
Test workspace_id metadata does not invoke isolated workspace or merge-back behavior.
acceptance: Targeted tests cover multi-lease state, batch planning, resource locks, serial safety, and compatibility single dispatch.
Negative tests cover production single-dispatch gating and workspace_id metadata-only behavior.
Regression suite still passes.

worker_split.notes: Do not edit workflow-engine, UI renderers, or cli.ps1.
Do not change Get-NextAction behavior except through shared helpers that preserve current single-dispatch output.
Use workflow helper functions if present; do not duplicate scheduler policy.

parent_owned: Any small cli.ps1 lease event projection if needed.
Any cli.ps1 edits so worker scopes stay disjoint.
Final targeted and full regression checks.
global_acceptance: Plans exist as Markdown and JSON under docs/plans.
The middle reviewer passes the task list before implementation.
Existing prep and conservative scheduler tests pass.
The repo can represent and plan multiple concurrent task-lane leases under explicit task_lane.mode=parallel, but production provider execution remains gated.
Existing cli.ps1 step cannot dispatch or execute more than one provider job even when fixture state uses task_lane.mode=parallel.
Batch lease candidates are derived read-only and are not persisted as ready_queue or auto-leased by production paths.

do_not_do: Do not make parallel mode default.
Do not implement isolated workspace or merge-back.
Do not touch unrelated dirty files.
llm_execution_guidance.verification_policy: Workers may run targeted tests for their slice, but parent runs final targeted and full regression checks.
